screenshots.note: Screenshots reused from QA #53 (Chromium launch fails in current environment due to pthread resource limits). Live site confirmed reachable via curl — same asset hashes as dist/.

carry_overs_unresolved.issue: overflow: visible on .office-floor — speech bubble, coffee corner, window elements escape bounds
carry_overs_unresolved.note: Office.css line 84 still has `overflow: visible`. Desktop screenshot confirms: Bloberto's speech bubble floats above the floor top edge, coffee corner label bleeds off the right side. Strike 9.
App.jsx lines 849, 871, and 1080 still register three separate visibilitychange listeners. Confirmed unchanged. Strike 9.
App.jsx lines 1129–1147 still contain a 12-property inline style object on the completionToast div. plantToast in Office.jsx still uses inline styles too. Strike 9.

carry_overs_unresolved.issue: VIBE_LABELS and vibeRanking defined inside useEffect
carry_overs_unresolved.note: App.jsx lines 991 and 1000 still define vibeRanking and VIBE_LABELS as fresh object literals on every useEffect execution. These are pure constants. Strike 9.
Office.jsx lines 1271–1272 (full-sync-banner, full-idle-banner) and line 1430 (vibe-caption) still mount/unmount with aria-live. App.jsx line 1128 (completionToast) same issue. Strike 9.

suggestions.title: Floor overflow still not contained — strike 9, please just fix this
suggestions.description: Office.css line 84: `overflow: visible` on .office-floor. Every review since #46 has flagged this. The desktop screenshot shows Bloberto's speech bubble floating a full 40px above the floor's top border. The coffee corner label 'COFFEE TRIPLE SHOT' bleeds past the right edge. Wander keyframes push idle workers to top: 85% — near enough to the bottom edge that their avatars partially exit the floor. Fix: change to `overflow: hidden` and handle the speech bubble by rendering it outside the floor container (e.g., as a sibling positioned relative to a wrapper). The solo-badge uses `top: -14px` — move it to a portal or wrapper outside the floor.

suggestions.title: Triple visibilitychange listeners — strike 9
suggestions.description: App.jsx registers three independent document.addEventListener('visibilitychange') calls: line 849 (polling resume), line 871 (poll interval adjustment), line 1080 (tab title animation). All three fire on every visibility change. Merge into a single listener in the polling useEffect that calls all three concerns. The title animation handler (line 1080) is in its own useEffect and already handles both hidden and visible states — collapse the resume listener (line 849) into it.

suggestions.title: Inline toast styles on completionToast — strike 9
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1129–1147 define 12 CSS properties inline (position, bottom, left, transform, background, border, borderRadius, padding, fontSize, fontWeight, color, boxShadow, zIndex, whiteSpace, pointerEvents, animation). The dynamic parts are just the border color and box-shadow glow. Extract everything static to a .completion-toast class in App.css, and reduce the inline style to only: `style={{ '--toast-color': completionToast.type === 'info' ? '#6b7280' : completionToast.color || '#6366f1' }}`. Same fix needed for plantToast in Office.jsx.

suggestions.title: VIBE_LABELS and vibeRanking inside useEffect — strike 9
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 991 defines `const vibeRanking = { 'after-hours': 0, ... }` and line 1000 defines `const VIBE_LABELS = { ... }` inside a useEffect that runs on every teamVibe.key change. These are pure constant lookup tables — zero dependency on component state or props. Hoist both to module scope (alongside VIBE_COLORS and VIBE_RANK already defined at lines 383–390). One-line fix each.

suggestions.title: aria-live on conditionally mounted elements — strike 9
suggestions.description: Screen readers announce aria-live region changes only when the region is already in the DOM. Three patterns still violate this: (1) completionToast div at App.jsx line 1128 — only mounted when `completionToast` is truthy; (2) full-sync-banner and full-idle-banner at Office.jsx lines 1271–1272 — conditional renders; (3) vibe-caption at Office.jsx line 1430 — only rendered when `vibeCaption` is truthy. Fix: render a persistent sr-only container with aria-live and swap its text content. The sr-only span at Office.jsx line 1237 already demonstrates the correct pattern. Apply it everywhere.

suggestions.title: Title animation timer leaks on rapid tab switch — new finding
suggestions.description: In the tab-title useEffect (App.jsx line 1039), the `handleVisibilityChange` handler reassigns the local `timer` variable with a new setInterval when the tab becomes visible again. The cleanup function `return () => { clearInterval(timer) }` captures `timer` by closure reference, so it will clear the LAST created interval. However, if the tab goes hidden, then immediately visible, then hidden again before the effect re-runs (i.e., before activeWorkers changes), the second hidden event calls `clearInterval(timer)` on the interval created in the first visible event — but the first hidden event ALREADY called clearInterval. Now timer is cleared twice (harmless), but the reassigned `timer` from the most recent visible event is not tracked. If this cycle repeats 3+ times between data polling cycles, orphaned intervals accumulate. Fix: use a ref to always hold the current timer ID instead of a closure-captured let variable.

suggestions.title: Footer quote span is a mouse-only interactive element — keyboard inaccessible
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1392: the footer tagline is a `<span>` with `onClick` and `style={{ cursor: 'pointer' }}` but no `role`, no `tabIndex`, no `onKeyDown`, and no `aria-label`. Keyboard users cannot discover or activate this Easter egg. Fix is one line: add `role="button" tabIndex={0} onKeyDown={e => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); /* same onClick logic */ } }} aria-label="Click for another Bloberto thought"`. Alternatively, convert the span to a `<button>` and style it inline.

suggestions.title: WORKER_WANDER_TOOLTIPS object defined inside .map() callback — allocates on every render
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1502 defines the WORKER_WANDER_TOOLTIPS lookup object (5 keys, 3 values each) inside the idleWorkers.map() callback. This allocates a new object on every render pass, for every idle worker. Same for the fallback anonymous wander tooltip map at lines 1511–1516. Both are pure constants. Hoist to module scope alongside WORKER_AWAY_MESSAGES and similar constants. Zero logic change, prevents unnecessary GC pressure on every render.

suggestions.title: blobFace() inner function recreated on every CharacterAvatar render
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 180 defines `const blobFace = () => { ... }` as an inner function inside the CharacterAvatar memo component. Even though memo prevents re-renders when props haven't changed, the component body still runs on its own renders and recreates blobFace. Extract as a named component `BlobFace({ vibeKey })` outside CharacterAvatar, or (simpler) replace with a static JSX lookup object keyed by vibeKey at module scope. This also makes the face rendering logic more readable and independently testable.

suggestions.title: Duplicate CSS vibe attribute selectors — single-quote vs double-quote rules still unresolved
suggestions.description: Office.css still has duplicate vibe-keyed selectors: single-quote variants at ~lines 91–95 set border and box-shadow, then double-quote variants at ~lines 97–115 add --grid-line custom properties for the same vibes. Both are valid CSS — browsers treat attribute selectors with single or double quotes identically — but the duplication means any specificity change must be made twice and the competing rules create ambiguity. Consolidate into a single set of selectors. Saves ~10 lines per vibe state.

suggestions.title: Mobile Office tab label clipped — 'Office' tab gets cut at 375px viewport
suggestions.description: On 375px mobile, the Office tab button renders truncated ('Offic' — visible in screenshot). The tab-toggle container has no overflow handling and the emoji + text at 0.85rem + 7px/20px padding causes clipping. The Dashboard tab doesn't render at all in the initial viewport. Fix: add `overflow-x: auto; -webkit-overflow-scrolling: touch;` on .tab-toggle for mobile, or reduce padding to 5px 12px at max-width: 420px. Alternatively, show just emoji on mobile: '🏢' and '📊' with a visually hidden text span.

suggestions.title: window.matchMedia() called inline during JSX render — not idiomatic and won't update
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1181 calls `window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)').matches` directly inside JSX to determine 'Tap to refresh' vs 'Move mouse to refresh' label on the polling-paused pill. Office.jsx line 673 does the same for '(hover: none)'. This is a side effect in render that (1) queries the DOM on every render, (2) won't reactively update if the user connects/disconnects an input device during the session, and (3) can throw in SSR contexts. Wrap in a `useMediaQuery` custom hook that attaches a MediaQueryList change listener. Both call sites benefit.

suggestions.title: lone-survivor-label has 7 inline style properties — move to CSS
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1493 applies a full presentational style block inline on `.lone-survivor-label`: `{ fontSize: '0.45rem', opacity: 0.6, position: 'absolute', bottom: '-0.6rem', left: '50%', transform: 'translateX(-50%)', whiteSpace: 'nowrap', color: 'rgba(255,255,255,0.7)' }`. All properties are static. Add `.lone-survivor-label` to Office.css. The only potentially dynamic property is color — if it ever needs to react to theme, a CSS custom property handles it better than inline style.

suggestions.title: VibeSparkline is empty on cold load — flashes blank before populating
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 684 initializes `vibeHistory` as `[]`. The useEffect that reads from localStorage and sets `vibeHistory` (line 1011) fires after the first render, triggered by `teamVibe.key`. This means the sparkline renders one empty frame before history loads. On a cold load with no localStorage data, the sparkline stays empty for the session. No visual indicator distinguishes 'empty history' from 'no history available'. Consider: (1) initialize state directly from localStorage using a lazy initializer `useState(() => { try { return JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem(VIBE_HISTORY_KEY)) || [] } catch { return [] } })`, or (2) show a 'first visit' placeholder dot.

suggestions.title: Two ambient-particle layers active simultaneously — App-level + Office-level
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1103 renders 12 ambient particles at the App root level (visible regardless of tab). Office.jsx line 1583 renders 7 more ambient particles inside the office-floor. On the Office tab, both layers animate simultaneously — 19 total CSS animation instances. On the Dashboard tab, the Office particles are hidden (floor not rendered) but the App-level 12 still animate. Confirm both layers are intentional and document them. If the office-floor particles are purely decorative duplicates of the App-level ones, remove the Office-level set to halve the animation count on the Office view.

suggestions.title: Vibe weather widget overlaps idle workers near bottom of floor
suggestions.description: The `.vibe-weather` element is positioned in the lower-right area of the floor. Wander keyframes send idle workers to top: 73–85% of the floor height. At the right viewport widths, wandering characters can drift behind or overlap the weather widget with no z-index resolution. The wander-2 path at 65% keyframe puts a worker at left: 71%, top: 82% — close to where the weather label typically sits. Add `z-index: 2` to `.vibe-weather` and ensure it's above wander characters, or adjust the wander paths to avoid the bottom-right region.

suggestions.title: ShortcutToast uses aria-live on conditionally unmounted element — same pattern as carry-overs
suggestions.description: ShortcutToast (App.jsx line 641) returns `null` when `phase === 'hidden'`, removing the `aria-live` div from the DOM. When the toast transitions to 'visible', screen readers may not announce the text reliably since the region wasn't present before content was injected. Keep the container always in the DOM; toggle visibility via CSS opacity/height instead of conditional rendering.

suggestions.title: polling-paused pill has onClick for mouse users but is not semantically interactive
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1178: the polling-paused pill is a `<span>` with `onClick={onResume}`. Like the footer tagline, it has no `role`, `tabIndex`, or keyboard handler, so keyboard-only users cannot activate it to resume polling. Convert to `<button>` or add `role="button" tabIndex={0}` with a keydown handler. The component already has `title` and `aria-live` — it's 90% accessible, just missing the interaction semantics.

suggestions.title: Quote counter in footer uses inline styles for opacity and fontVariantNumeric
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1396: the quote progress indicator `{quoteBonus % ...}/{BONUS_TAGLINES.length + 1}` uses `style={{ opacity: 0.55, fontSize: '0.7em', fontVariantNumeric: 'tabular-nums' }}` inline. This is static presentation — extract to a `.footer-quote-counter` class in App.css. Minor, but consistent with the codebase's otherwise reasonable CSS discipline.

suggestions.title: Activity log emoji fallback '🤖' for unmatched workers — confusing for system events
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 96: entries without a roster match fall through to `ROLE_EMOJIS[workerData?.role] ?? (entry.worker ? '🤖' : null)`. Auto-generated entries like vibe transitions or system pings that have no matching worker show a robot emoji 🤖 which implies a bot/automation but doesn't communicate the event type. Consider mapping `entry.type === 'system'` explicitly to ⚙️ and reserving 🤖 for genuinely automated worker entries.
